title: Adapter
description: Translate one interface to another to integrate legacy/third-party code.

cost.costNotes: Seeded via heuristics; validate against current pricing and expected workload (throughput, storage, retention, egress).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: The Adapter pattern is a structural design pattern (GOF) that has no direct infrastructure or service costs. It's a code organization technique that adds a thin wrapper class to translate one interface to another. There are no cloud resources, databases, or external services required - just application code. Any runtime costs are negligible (measured in microseconds of CPU time per adapter call) and absorbed into normal application hosting costs.
provenance.adoptionCost: Very low adoption cost. The Adapter pattern is one of the simplest GOF patterns to implement. With modern AI coding assistants, implementation typically requires: 1-2 hours to identify integration points and design the adapter interface, 2-3 hours to implement the adapter class(es) with proper delegation and interface translation, 1-2 hours for unit testing. Total: ~4-7 hours at $50-75/hour = $200-500. The pattern has minimal learning curve for developers familiar with basic OOP principles (interfaces, composition, delegation). No specialized expertise required. Risk is low since adapters are isolated and don't affect existing code.
provenance.licenseCost: Zero license cost. The Adapter pattern is a well-established, open design pattern from the Gang of Four (1994) with no proprietary ownership or licensing requirements. It can be implemented in any programming language using standard language features (classes, interfaces, inheritance). No special tools, frameworks, or commercial libraries are required. Any IDE or text editor is sufficient for implementation.

provides.reasoning: The Adapter pattern's primary purpose is to make incompatible interfaces work together by translating one interface to another. This capability directly represents the pattern's core function of bridging interface mismatches between components.

The pattern explicitly enables integration of legacy and third-party code by wrapping their interfaces to make them compatible with modern systems. This is a primary use case mentioned in the pattern description.

The pattern provides interface translation capability by transforming method calls from one interface format to another. This emphasizes the active transformation aspect of converting between incompatible interfaces.

requires.reasoning: The Adapter pattern requires at least one existing interface (either the adaptee's interface or the target interface) to wrap or translate. Without an existing interface to adapt from or to, the pattern cannot function.
The pattern only makes sense when there's an interface mismatch to resolve. Without incompatible interfaces that need bridging, there is no adaptation problem for this pattern to solve.

supports_nfr.reason: Adapter organises code behavior by translating interfaces, not data content; it applies regardless of whether PII is present in the system.
Adapter is a structural code pattern; GDPR compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of GDPR requirements.
Adapter is a structural code pattern; CCPA compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of CCPA requirements.
Adapter is a structural code pattern; HIPAA compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of HIPAA requirements.
Adapter is a structural code pattern; SOX compliance obligations apply to the system, not to this pattern specifically — it is applicable regardless of SOX requirements.
Adapter operates below the persistence layer, translating interfaces between components; read-your-writes consistency is enforced at the data store, not by this pattern.
Adapter does not implement persistence; strict durability guarantees are provided at the infrastructure layer and are orthogonal to this interface-translation pattern.

supports_constraints.reason: Adapter is a language-level pattern that works across all cloud environments.
Adapter is a GoF pattern implementable in all object-oriented and procedural languages.
Adapter pattern is applicable to any platform type.

adapter_type.description: Object adapter uses composition (wraps adaptee instance), class adapter uses inheritance (multiple inheritance where supported). Object adapter is more flexible, class adapter can override adaptee behavior.

interface_compatibility.description: One-way adapter only translates from legacy to new interface. Two-way adapter allows bidirectional communication, useful when both systems need to interact.

legacy_preservation.description: Whether to preserve the original legacy interface alongside the adapted one, allowing gradual migration vs forcing immediate adoption of new interface.
